model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: Michigan is the pick because the underlying efficiency gap is still sizable: Michigan is #2 in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite defense, while Georgia sits around the low-30s/high-30s and is much weaker defensively. Georgia can absolutely win if its top-20 caliber offense gets hot from three and it forces Michigan into turnovers, but Michigan’s ability to generate stops and create higher-quality looks gives it more reliable paths to control the game. The main watch item is Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg’s ankle, but reporting indicates he’s expected to play, keeping Michigan’s baseline advantage intact.

reasoningStep.title: Midwest Round of 32 — Michigan vs Georgia
reasoningStep.summary: Michigan advances on a clear two-way efficiency edge and a defensive matchup advantage, with only modest injury-related uncertainty.
evidence: Ratings snapshot: Michigan (KenPom #2, AdjEM +38.38; Torvik #2) vs Georgia (KenPom #31, AdjEM +20.47; Torvik #37).
Defense gap is the separator: Michigan KenPom AdjD 88.4 vs Georgia AdjD 104.2 (Georgia’s offense is strong, but its stop-making is the more exploitable unit).
Availability context: Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg has been dealing with an ankle sprain but has been reported as expected to play (monitor pregame status).
Upset path acknowledged: Georgia’s credible route is shooting/turnover variance, but it’s harder to sustain without consistent defensive stops against Michigan’s elite offense.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
